name: Metastatic malignant neoplasm
description: Metastatic malignant neoplasm — cancer that has spread to distant sites; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.

en.name: Metastatic malignant neoplasm
en.shortDescription: Metastatic malignant neoplasm — cancer that has spread to distant sites; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

A metastatic malignant neoplasm is a cancer that has spread (metastasised) from its primary site to distant organs. In intensive care, metastatic disease is a major prognostic comorbidity and informs goals-of-care discussions. Metastatic cancer is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *432851 / 128462008 — Metastatic malignant neoplasm* with all descendants included, capturing secondary/metastatic malignant neoplasms across sites.

## Mapping Notes

- This set captures the **metastatic** state. A localised (non-metastatic) primary cancer is conceptually distinct and does not, by itself, map here.
- Haematologic malignancies (leukemia, lymphoma, myeloma) have their own sets and are not "metastatic solid tumours".
